danger_signals.visual_form: Magic Crystal item appears in top-left HUD during enemy-infested areas; white dots move toward center
danger_signals.meaning: Enemies are present and approaching; combat is imminent if player does not evade

danger_signals.signal: White dot reaching center on radar
danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy dot converges with player position (center) on crystal ball display
danger_signals.meaning: Battle will break out immediately; combat unavoidable

danger_signals.visual_form: Overworld transitions to dungeon areas; visual distinction from safe city zones
danger_signals.meaning: High enemy density; player must navigate quickly to avoid forced combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Appearance of rival playable characters in overworld/dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Potential duel for runes; player character may lose rune(s) and money if defeated

danger_signals.visual_form: Persistent NPC enemy visually distinct from standard encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Hired assassin actively hunting player; represents traitor apprentice's threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Safe area environment with NPCs, inns, shops; no crystal ball radar active
safety_signals.meaning: No random combat; player can freely explore, rest, trade, recruit allies

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated building or zone within cities
safety_signals.meaning: Safe recovery point; player can heal and prepare for next expedition

safety_signals.signal: Absence of white dots on crystal ball
safety_signals.visual_form: Crystal ball radar shows only player, terrain, and dungeon/rune markers; no enemy dots
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window; enemies not yet in proximity

safety_signals.visual_form: Second character appears alongside player in exploration and battle
safety_signals.meaning: Player has convinced/recruited another apprentice to assist; increased combat power

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystal Ball item (distributed by King Lemele)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each of the six rival apprentices has a distinct character model (human, elf, dwarf, robot, demon, alien)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear throughout dungeons and overworld; may occupy rune locations as guards or competitors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal authority; palace location; seven runes as symbolic power source
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Lemele appears as benevolent ruler; Gorsia revealed as true antagonist with destructive divine power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palace serves as recruitment and training hub; runes scattered as quest objectives across world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Independent assassin hired by traitor apprentice (randomized identity per playthrough)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct NPC model; persistent pursuer differentiating from standard enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears unexpectedly during quest progression; represents hidden traitor threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual rune artifacts; regional power bases (wizards, kings, dragons)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier unique enemy encounters; thematically distinct by possession type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupy major dungeons and cities throughout Ticondera; must be confronted to obtain runes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Advanced technology; robot units; city of Melenam
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical enemies; super-Tetsujin as rogue antagonist
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ruins of Melenam explorable early game; revealed as technologically advanced city in past timeline

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game: Lemele appears as benevolent ruler dispatching apprentices
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Palace setting; formal recruitment scene; distribution of crystal balls as tools
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Apprentices cooperate in training; world appears stable under wise leadership

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-game: Quest progression through rune collection
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overworld exploration with crystal ball radar; dungeon encounters with tyrants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Apprentices compete and may duel for runes; bounty hunter hunts player; tyrants resist rune surrender

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game revelation: Lemele is Gorsia; runes contain trapped divine power
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemele's true form revealed; runes destroyed; dramatic lightning/divine effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Apprentices' quest revealed as deception; Gorsia absorbs power and transports player to past

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-game past timeline: Ticondera 5,000 years ago during original Gorsia conflict
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Melenam appears as technologically advanced city (not ruins); Saro alive; rogue Tetsujin active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player witnesses destruction of Melenam; observes Saro's fatal wounding by Gorsia; receives runes from Saro's disciples

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Endgame: Player seals Gorsia's power with seven runes
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ritual/magical sealing effects; final boss encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player defeats Gorsia using rune power; world cycles or concludes depending on outcome

environmental_storytelling.example: Crystal ball radar appears only in enemy-infested areas, signaling danger zones vs. safe towns; no radar in cities conveys safety
Melenam appears as ancient ruins in early game; later revealed as thriving technologically advanced city in past timeline, showing world's cyclical destruction

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character visual distinctness for faction identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Seven apprentices span human, elf, dwarf, robot, demon, alien types; visual variety immediately communicates diversity of King Lemele's recruitment across globe
Player can convince other apprentices to partner mid-quest; visual transition from rival to ally reflects shifting political allegiances
Each rune occupies a unique dungeon or city ruled by thematic boss (wizard, king, dragon); environmental design ties power source to local authority
Pison's repeated appearances throughout quest progression reinforce hidden traitor threat without spoiling which apprentice hired them

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can walk freely, interact with NPCs, rest, duel other apprentices, recruit allies
Must move quickly, avoid white dots on radar, grab chests, navigate to objectives before combat forced
Move quickly and randomly on crystal ball radar toward player; initiate combat on contact
Can duel player for runes; may join as allies if convinced; always progress own rune collection in parallel
Pursues player persistently; represents traitor apprentice's threat; attacks with assassination intent
Occupy strongholds; resist rune surrender through combat; thematically unique encounter design
Appears benevolent, wise, powerful; distributes crystal balls; recruits and trains apprentices
Destroys runes, re-absorbs power, uses divine lightning, transports player to past; actively hostile

notes: The 7th Saga uses the crystal ball radar as the primary mechanical and visual vehicle for communicating world danger state. Safe areas (cities) show no radar and allow free exploration; dangerous areas activate the radar and introduce time-pressure gameplay. The game's central narrative twist—Lemele as Gorsia—is communicated through dramatic revelation rather than early visual foreshadowing, making the late-game visual shift (Lemele's transformation, rune destruction) a key storytelling moment. Faction identity is conveyed primarily through character visual design (seven distinct races/types) rather than color coding or heraldry. Temporal storytelling is achieved by revealing Melenam's dual nature (ruins vs. thriving city), communicating cyclical world destruction. The randomized traitor apprentice identity creates emergent political tension without requiring complex visual differentiation systems.
